questionHtml:
This morning, Billy walked one kilometre up a hill, and it took 22 minutes.

Explain why it would be inappropriate to use the equation found in part (a) to estimate the gradient of this hill.
markschemeHtml:
EITHER

using the T on G regression line cannot (always) reliably make a prediction for G            R1

OR

equation is for Time on Gradient not Gradient on Time.            R1

OR

this estimate is an extrapolation            R1

OR

there is no reason to assume this new hill has constant gradient            R1

[1 mark]

meta.title: Explain why it would be inappropriate to use the equation found in part (a) to estimate the gradient of this hill.
breadcrumbLabels: SL 4.4—Pearsons, scatter diagrams, eqn of y on x
